name: Peasant Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixer bowl, combine 2 cups of the all-purpose flour and the yeast.
2. In a saucepan, heat water, molasses, oil, and salt just until warm (115 to 120 degrees). Stir constantly.
3. Add the mixture to the flour and yeast mixture.
4. Beat on low speed of an electric mixer for 1/2 minute, scraping the bowl.
5. Beat on high speed for 3 minutes.
6. Stir in rye flour, bran, cornmeal, caraway seeds, and as much remaining all-purpose flour as you can mix in with a spoon.
7. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead enough remaining all-purpose flour to make a moderately stiff dough that is smooth and elastic (6 to 8 minutes total).
8. Shape into a ball. Place in a greased bowl; turn once to grease the surface. Cover; let rise in a warm place until double (1 to 1 1/4 hours).
9. Punch down; turn out onto a floured surface. Divide in half.
10. Cover; let rest for 10 minutes.
11. Shape into 2 loaves and place in 2 greased 8x4x2-inch loaf pans.
12. Cover; let rise until nearly doubled (30 to 45 minutes).
13. Bake in a 375 degree oven for 35 to 40 minutes or until the bread is done. Cool on a wire rack. Makes 2 loaves, or 36 servings.
